w:r.w:t: The following Software Requirements Specification has been accepted and approved by the following:
Sequence Diagram User Registration and Login
4.1.2 Sequence Diagram Mood Tracking and Analysis
4.2.2 DFD level 1 User management & mood tracking
4.2.3 DFD level 1 AI chat & appointment booking
Appendix A.1 Mental Health Support Systems
Appendix A.2 Artificial Intelligence in Mental Health
Appendix A.3 Mood Tracking and Behavioral Analytics
Appendix A.4 – Data Privacy and Security in Mental Health Systems

The Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document provides a complete description of the functional and non-functional requirements of the
Health Support System. This document explains what the system must do, ensuring that the designers and develope
expected behavior. This SRS focuses only on requirements, not design or implementation details.
The purpose of this SRS is to clearly define the requirements of the

Health Support System, a web-based platform designed to support users in monitoring, improving, and understanding their mental health through personalized tools such as mood tracking, self-assessment quizzes, AI-assisted chat, and therapist appointment booking. This document is intended for:
Developers and Designers: to understand system features and build the application accordingly.
Project Supervisors and Evaluators: to review the project goals, scope, and planned functionalities.
Testers: to verify that the final product meets all stated requirements.
Stakeholders and End Users: to understand what the system will provide.
The software product to be developed is titled
Provide users with self-assessment quizzes to evaluate stress, anxiety, or emotional states.
Offer mood tracking features with visual insights and daily mood logs.
Enable AI-assisted chat for basic emotional support and guidance.
Allow users to book appointments with mental-health professionals.
Maintain user profiles, login authentication, and secure data handling.
Present educational resources, tips, and self-care content related to mental well-being.
Replace certified psychologists or offer medical diagnosis.
Function as a real-time crisis management platform.
Help individuals better understand their emotional patterns.
Provide an accessible, stigma-free environment for mental wellness.
Offer AI-supported guidance for everyday emotional challenges.
Connect users with professionals when deeper support is needed.
Empower users through consistent tracking, reflection, and personalized insights.
The scope described here summarizes the overall objectives and benefits of the system without listing individual requirements, which are detailed in later sections.
Below are the definitions and abbreviations used within this SRS:
CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete operations
User Mood Log: Daily mood entries stored in the system
Self-Assessment Quiz: A questionnaire used to evaluate emotional well-being
Dashboard: Main page showing user’s mood trends, recent activities, and suggestions
Therapist Appointment: A scheduled consultation with a mental-health professional
Frontend: Client-side interface built using React.js
Backend: Server-side logic built using Node.js and Express.js
MongoDB: Database used to store system data
The following resources were consulted during the preparation of this SRS:
IEEE Software Requirements Specification Standards – IEEE Computer Society.
React.js Official Documentation – Meta Platforms Inc.
Academic articles and online resources related to mental health assessment tools
These references can be obtained from their official websites or institutional libraries.
This SRS is organized into several major sections to ensure clarity and structured
Provides an overview of the system, its purpose, scope, and key terms.
Describes users, system environment, product funct
Provides detailed functional and non-functional requirements, including system
Includes supporting information such as definitions, diagrams, and reference
materials. Readers interested in how the system behaves should focus on Section 2, while developers
implementing the system should carefully review Section 3 for complete requirement details.
This section provides a general description of the
System. It explains the background, environment, major functions, user characteristics,
constraints, and assumptions that influence the overall requirements of the system. The
purpose of this section is to help customers and stakeholders understand, in simple
language, what the system will do and the context in which it will operate.
Detailed technical requirements are presented later in Section 3.
Health Support System is an independent and self-contained
web application. It is not part of a larger system; however, it interacts with external services
such as AI-based sentiment analysis APIs (e.g., OpenAI), email services, and optional
The system is designed to fill a growing need in the digital mental health sector by providing emotionally supportive tools, such as:
features such as therapy booking or meditation,
integrated platform, combining assessment tools, personalized mood insights, AI-assisted
conversation, and a smooth user experience focused on accessibility and comfort.
does not replace professional medical evaluation,
but supports users in tracking and understanding their emotional trends.
The web application will act as a black box, receiving user inputs and providing emotional
support outputs without exposing internal implementation details.
This subsection explains the normal and special operational conditions of the system:
Interactive Mode: When users log in, complete quizzes, chat with AI, track
Unattended Mode: When the system stores logs, generates mood insights,
and updates data automatically in the background.
Most operations are user-driven and occur during interactive usage.
Some automated tasks (such as daily mood reminders or data backups) may
The system will process quiz results, analyze mood logs, and store user data
securely using backend APIs and a database.
AI-service calls will process text to analyze mood trends and provide
Database backup schedules may be set (daily/weekly) to ensure data
Automatic recovery handling ensures system continuity even after
These operations ensure smooth system behavior and influence backend design choices.
Ease system is fully web-based, the site adaptation requirements are
A stable internet connection at the user’s location.
Proper environment setup on server side (Node.js, database access,
Required API keys for external AI-based services (e.g., OpenAI).
The backend must be deployed on a secure hosting server.
A MongoDB database instance must be configured before system activation.
Environment variables (API keys, DB URI, server port) must be set correctly.
There are no special hardware installation requirements such as generators or cooling
Ease is not installed physically at a customer site; it operates
Health Support System performs several major functions that
Users create accounts and authenticate securely.
Stress, anxiety, and mood quizzes help users identify emotional states.
Users record daily moods which are later visualized using graphs/trends.
AI-based chat provides personalized wellness messages and helpful
Users view therapist profiles and schedule online appointments.
Displays suggested resources, weekly mood summary, recent activity, and
Articles, self-help guides, breathing exercises, and mental health tips.
These functions work together to create a supportive, user-friendly mental wellness platform.
The intended users of the Mind
People seeking emotional support or wanting to track their mood.
Users may not be highly technical, so UI must be simple and intuitive.
Need quick, accessible tools to manage stress and academic pressure.
Require an easy interface for managing bookings.
The UI must be simple, calm, and minimalistic to avoid overwhelming users.
Text should be clear and non-technical.
Color choices must be soothing to create a positive emotional environment.
Navigation must be intuitive for users with basic computer skills.
Several constraints influence the design and development of the Mind
The system must respect user privacy and ethical guidelines (e.g., user
None for end users; any device with a browser is sufficient.
AI and email APIs must be accessible.
Multiple users may use the system simultaneously; concurrency must be
Logs for user activity and system operations should be maintained.
Data encryption, secure authentication, and restricted API access.
System should be available with minimal downtime.
Not a medical emergency system → must clearly state this to users.
These constraints help determine limits and mandatory considerations in the system.
The system relies on certain assumptions and external conditions:
Users have internet access and a functional device.
The hosting server supports Node.js and MongoDB.
External AI API services remain available and stable.
Browsers support modern web standards (JavaScript, HTML5).
Therapists who join the platform manage their availability schedules.
Users provide accurate information during assessments.
Email or SMS service for notifications remains active.
If any of these assumptions change, the SRS may need to be updated accordingly.
This section describes the detailed functional and non-functional requirements of the
Ease Mental Health Support System. Each requirement is uniquely identified,
testable, traceable, and written at a level that can guide developers during system design,
This section does not describe how the system will be designed; it describes what the
The system interacts with several external services and components. Each interface is listed
below along with its purpose and expected behavior.
The system must integrate with an AI API (e.g., OpenAI API) to perform text-based
sentiment analysis for the AI chat and mood prediction feature.
Sends user-provided text and receives analyzed sentiment or AI-generated
To provide personalized and adaptive emotional support.
The system must connect with a mail service (e.g.,
appointment confirmations and password reset emails.
Notify users about appointments, account activities, and reminders.
The system must store user data, quiz results, mood entries, chat logs, and
Provide persistent, secure, and scalable data management.
The system will be accessed through a web-based Graphical User Interface (GUI)
Intuitive navigation with a top Navbar and left-side Dashboard panel.
Simple, calming color theme suitable for mental health applications.
Large buttons and readable typography for accessibility.
Interactive visual elements (charts, progress bars, cards).
Support both desktop and mobile devices (responsive design).
Present accurate error messages (invalid inputs, blank fields).
Include focus indicators for screen-reader compatibility.
Support accessible color contrast for users with visual impairment.
Use clear and friendly microcopy (“How are you feeling today?” instead of technical language).
Users interact with forms, quizzes, charts, and chat windows.
All interactions occur through point-and-click and keyboard navigation.
The system has no direct hardware interface requirements.
Ease is a web-based software application, it does not communicate with or
The only requirement is that users access the system through standard computing devices
(desktop, laptop, tablet, smartphone) with an internet connection.
The system depends on several external software products and APIs to operate correctly.
Purpose: Provides routing and middleware support for API endpoints.
Purpose: Database to store all system data.
Interface: JSON-based documents through Mongoose ORM.
Purpose: Frontend UI rendering and component management.
Purpose: AI-driven emotional support, mood prediction, and message generation.
Response: JSON containing sentiment or response text.
Purpose: Display mood trends and insights in visual form.
Purpose: Provide consistent design and responsive layouts.
The system relies on standard communication protocols:
All requests between frontend, backend, and APIs occur through secure
Required at networking level for all client-server communication.
Used for account notifications and reminders.
If real-time chat is added in future updates,
Not required in the first version.
Since the system relies on widely used standard web protocols, no custom communication
This section defines the core functional requirements of the Mind
Ease Mental Health Support System. Each feature is described in detail, including its inputs, internal processing, outputs, and error handling mechanisms.
This feature allows users to create accounts, log in securely, and access personalized
features such as quizzes, chat, and mood tracking.
Authenticate user by verifying email + hashed password.
Generate secure session token or JWT.
This feature allows users to complete mental health self-assessment quizzes (stress,
Calculate score based on predefined scoring rules.
Suggested recommendations (tips, resources, or chat option)
Missing answer for a required question
Allows users to record daily moods and view emotional trends.
This feature enables users to chat with an AI assistant that provides supportive, empathetic responses.
Send message to AI sentiment/response API.
Allows users to browse therapists and book counseling appointments.
Provides users with access to mental health resources.
User selects a category or article
Each use case represents a key functional interaction between the user and the application.
The purpose of these use cases is to define how different types of users (Admin, Family Member) will achieve their goals within the system.
Account does not exist → suggest signup.
System sends message to AI API.
This section describes the major classes/objects of the
Each class represents a real-world entity of the system and is directly traceable to the functional requirements and use cases defined in Section 3.2 and 3.3.
Use Cases: User Registration, User Login
Use Cases: AI Mental Health Chat
Non-functional requirements define the quality attributes of the Mind
All requirements in this section are measurable, testable, and apply system-wide
95% of all user requests (login, quiz loading, mood entry, chat response) shall
AI chat responses shall be received within 3 seconds under normal load.
Backend API endpoints must complete processing within 500ms for standard
All UI pages should load within 2 seconds over a standard broadband
The system must support a minimum of 500 concurrent users without
The system shall maintain a reliability level of 99% uptime excluding
The system shall maintain a minimum MTBF of 30 days.
The system must recover from non-critical failures within 2 minutes.
Automatic reconnection to the database must occur if the connection is lost
System availability must be at least 99% over a 24-hour cycle.
Downtime shall not exceed 1 minute per day for automated deployments or
Users should be able to access their dashboard and personal data at all times
All secure operations require JWT-based authentication.
Passwords must be hashed using industry-standard hashing algorithms (
All communication between frontend, backend, and external APIs must use
Sensitive data stored in the database must use encryption at rest.
Only authorized users may access personal data, chat history, or
Admin users shall have restricted admin-panel access.
No user mental health data shall be shared without explicit user consent.
System must comply with basic digital privacy principles.
All user inputs must be validated to prevent SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, and other attacks.
The system shall be developed using modular components to support
Every major function must include internal comments and documentation.
The system must maintain a structured error log for debugging and maintenance.
All third-party libraries must be updated at least once every 6 months.
The system must support modern browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari.
The application must be fully responsive and accessible on mobile, tablet,
Ease should function on Windows, macOS, Linux,
The backend must be deployable on any cloud platform (AWS,
Inverse requirements describe what the system must NOT do (useful for clarity):
The system shall not provide medical diagnosis or replace professional mental
The system shall not offer emergency or crisis management services.
The system shall not share user data with third parties without explicit consent.
The system shall not allow unauthorized users to access personal data or chat
The system shall not store passwords in plain text.
Ease system requires structured data storage for users, quizzes, moods, chat
User data (name, email, password, profile info)
Quiz results → Weekly or as required
Users must only access their own data.
Admins may access therapist profiles and resources.
Unique email constraint for all users.
Appointment time must be unique per therapist slot.
Mood log must not be duplicated for same user & date.
Quiz results must include valid score and quiz ID.
User data retained until account deletion.
Mood logs retained for at least 1 year.
Chat messages retained for 6 months for user history.
Appointment logs retained for compliance for 1 year.
Design constraints refer to limitations or requirements that influence the
system must comply with the following standards:
JSON-based REST API must be used for all data exchange.
Database table/collection names must follow meaningful camelCase or
Consistent naming for UI components and API routes.
All updates to user data, bookings, or quiz results must be logged with
Must follow digital privacy principles similar to GDPR (where applicable).
User consent required before collecting personal data.
This section presents the analysis models used to understand, refine, and validate the
system requirements described in Section 3 of the SRS.
Each model provides a visual or narrative representation of how the system behaves,
processes data, and responds to user interactions.
These models help developers, testers, and stakeholders clearly understand system
behavior before the design phase begins.
Sequence Diagram Mood Tracking and Analysis
Sequence Diagram Mood Tracking and Analysis
DFD level 1 User management & mood tracking
DFD level 1 AI chat & appointment booking
DFD level 1 AI chat & appointment booking
This section provides additional information that supports and complements the requirements defined in this Software Requirements Specification (SRS).
It includes background research related to the project domain and a data dictionary describing key data elements used within the system.
This appendix presents background research conducted to understand the domain, technologies, and challenges relevant to the
Ease – AI-Based Mental Health Support System
The research helps justify design decisions and requirement definitions.
Appendix A.1 Mental Health Support Systems
Mental health support systems are digital platforms designed to assist users in managing stress, anxiety, depression, and emotional well-being.
Traditional mental health services face challenges such as limited accessibility, social stigma, and high costs. Digital platforms help overcome these barriers by providing:
Early detection of mental health issues
Recent studies show that web-based mental health platforms improve user engagement and provide effective preliminary support, especially for young adults and students.
Appendix A.2 Artificial Intelligence in Mental Health
Artificial Intelligence (AI) plays a significant role in modern mental health applications.
AI techniques such as Natural Language Processing (NLP) and sentiment analysis enable systems to:
Analyze user text to detect emotional states
Assist users through AI-powered chat support
AI does not replace professional therapists but serves as a supportive tool that enhances early intervention and self-awareness.
Appendix A.3 Mood Tracking and Behavioral Analytics
Mood tracking allows users to log their daily emotional states and observe long-term behavioral patterns.
By analyzing mood data over time, systems can generate insights such as:
When combined with AI-based analytics, mood tracking helps users better understand their mental health and
Appendix A.4 – Data Privacy and Security in Mental Health Systems
Mental health data is highly sensitive and requires strong privacy and security measures.
Common challenges include unauthorized access, data misuse, and lack of transparency.
Modern systems address these concerns by:
This appendix defines key data elements used within the
The data dictionary ensures consistency, clarity, and shared understanding among developers, testers, and stakeholders.
